origin.capability_match: Stake LP tokens (as PoL-eligible Receipt Tokens) in Reward Vaults

attack_surface.data_description: The $BGT emissions data flow (Data Flow 3) to the Reward Vault Contract is disrupted, or the Reward Vault contract itself is made unavailable for staking/claiming.

trust_boundary_risk.boundary_name: BeraHub - Reward Vault Smart Contract Boundary

security_gap.gap_description: The availability of the Reward Vault Contract for staking, withdrawals, and reward claims, as well as the inflow of rewards, is critical for LPs.

actor_risk.description: Liquidity Provider is unable to stake, withdraw, claim rewards, or their expected rewards do not materialize due to a DoS condition.

behavioral_risk.rationale: DoS against the Reward Vault or its funding mechanisms directly impacts LP participation and earnings.

assumptions: The Reward Vault Contract has a vulnerability (e.g., gas-intensive loop, reentrancy lock) that can be triggered to make it unusable.
The $BGT Emitter/Distributor (source of Data Flow 3) can be subject to DoS, halting $BGT flow.
Network congestion or targeted transaction spam can prevent LPs from interacting with the Reward Vault.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust smart contract development practices (e.g., gas limits, reentrancy guards), ensure resilient infrastructure for critical components like $BGT Emitter, and provide alternative interaction methods if UIs like BeraHub are down.

description: An attacker causes a Denial of Service against the Reward Vault Contract (Component 6) or its dependent data flows (e.g., Data Flow 3 for $BGT emissions). This prevents Liquidity Provider (Actor 4) from performing staking actions (Behavior 1), claiming rewards, or results in rewards not being distributed.

trace_source.doc_reference: pol/Reward Vaults (doc_25), pol/Block Production and Emissions (doc_20)
trace_source.matched_capability: Stake LP tokens (as PoL-eligible Receipt Tokens) in Reward Vaults
